content.problemStatement:
You are given a sortedsortedsorted list AAA of size NNN.You have to make a new list BBB such that B[i]B[i]B[i] is equal to the number of elements strictlystrictlystrictly greatergreatergreater than A[i]A[i]A[i] in the list AAA.

Print the newnewnew listlistlist.
constraints: 1≤T≤1001 \le T \le 1001≤T≤100 , where TTT is the number of test cases.
1≤N≤1001 \le N \le 1001≤N≤100, where NNN is the number of elements in the list.
1≤A[i]≤10000001 \le A[i] \le 10000001≤A[i]≤1000000 ,where A[i]A[i]A[i] is the ithithith element in the list given to you.
151515 pointspointspoints: All the elements in the list given to you are distinct.
353535 pointspointspoints: Original constraints: Elements can be repeated in the list.
content.inputFormat:
The first line consists of TTT, denoting the number of test cases.

First line of each test case consists of one integer denoting NNN, where NNN is the size of the list given to you.

Second line of each test case contains the list given to you containing NNN elements.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case print the list in a single line and the elements of the list should be separated by spacespacespace.

Print the answer to eacheacheach test case in a newnewnew linelineline.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The explanation for test case 1 of sample input :
The first element in the new list is 3 since the first element in the previous list was 1, and there are three elements which are strictly greater than 1, that is 2, 4 and 4.
The second element in the new list is 2 since the second element in the previous list was 2, and there are two elements which are strictly greater than 2, that is 4 and 4.
The third and fourth element in the new list is 0  since third and fourth element in the previous list was  4 and there are no elements which are strictly greater than them.
